problem.statement: Indie developers and small teams have no affordable webhook fanout API — existing tools (Svix, Hookdeck) start at $15–490/mo for features that are overkill at small scale.

evidence.summary: Cheapest paid plan starts at $490/mo — entirely out of reach for indie/small team use
$15–60/mo but no fanout to multiple endpoints on lower tiers
Scored 22/25 across market size, build feasibility, differentiation, revenue clarity, contextual fit

existing_solutions.name: Svix
existing_solutions.weakness: Priced for enterprises — completely inaccessible to indie/solo developers

existing_solutions.name: Hookdeck
existing_solutions.weakness: Limited fanout on lower tiers, no multi-provider signature verification bundle

existing_solutions.name: Self-hosted (DIY)
existing_solutions.weakness: Requires building retries, logging, sig verification, and fanout from scratch

primary.description: Indie developer or small engineering team (1–5 people) building a SaaS product that receives webhooks from providers like Stripe and GitHub
primary.job_to_be_done: When I receive a webhook from a provider / I want to fan it out to multiple endpoints with signature verification and automatic retries / so I can build reliable integrations without managing the infrastructure myself
primary.willingness_to_pay_notes: $9–29/mo based on competitor pricing gap — Svix starts at $490/mo, Hookdeck at $15/mo but with limited fanout. Target is the underserved $9–29/mo band.

distribution.notes: Show HN post — lead with the Svix price comparison, demo the API in the post
r/webdev and r/SideProject — focus on the $9/mo vs $490/mo angle
Add 'Powered by FanHook' badge + link for projects using the API

in.name: Sink management
in.description: POST /sinks to create a sink, GET /sinks to list, DELETE /sinks/:id to remove
in.rationale: Core primitive — a sink is the entry point for all incoming webhooks

in.name: Ingest + signature verification
in.description: Receive webhooks at /ingest/:sinkId, verify Stripe and GitHub signatures against raw payload
in.rationale: Directly serves JTBD — without sig verification the product is just a proxy, not a trusted relay

in.name: Fanout with retries
in.description: Fan out verified payload to up to 3 configured routes, 3 retry attempts with linear backoff
in.rationale: The core value proposition — this is what makes it a fanout API, not just a passthrough

in.name: Event log
in.description: GET /sinks/:id/events — last 50 events with status, timestamp, and route delivery results
in.rationale: Enables debugging — developers need to see what was received and where it was sent

in.name: API key auth
in.description: Bearer API key authentication on all /api routes, key generated on sink creation
in.rationale: Security baseline — no auth means any caller can read or delete sinks

out.name: Payload transformation
out.reason: Increases build complexity significantly — not required for core JTBD

out.name: Dead letter queue + redrive
out.reason: Valuable but not blocking for v0.1 — retries cover the basic reliability need

out.name: Non-Stripe/GitHub provider verification
out.reason: Additional providers need research into sig formats — scope risk for v0.1

out.name: Team seats and SSO
out.reason: Enterprise feature — not relevant to indie developer persona

out.name: Custom domains
out.reason: Infrastructure complexity out of scope for indie product at this stage

core_flow: Developer creates a sink via POST /sinks → receives sink URL + API key
Developer configures Stripe/GitHub to send webhooks to the sink URL
Provider fires a webhook → FanHook receives it at /ingest/:sinkId
FanHook verifies the provider signature against the raw payload
FanHook fans out the verified payload to all configured routes
Failed routes are retried up to 3 times with linear backoff
Developer inspects delivery results via GET /sinks/:id/events

additions: Stripe subscription checkout (Free → Starter upgrade flow)
Usage metering — track events/mo per sink against tier limits
Freemium enforcement — block ingest when Free tier limit (1k events/mo) is exceeded with a clear error response
Onboarding wizard — first-sink setup guide on /dashboard after signup
Landing page — hero, how it works, pricing table, CTA
non_negotiables: A user on the Free tier cannot exceed 1,000 events/mo — ingest must return 429 with upgrade prompt
Stripe checkout flow works end-to-end: click upgrade → checkout → webhook → tier updated
Landing page explains the product and pricing without requiring login
App still deploys and runs on Replit after all mmp additions
Payment webhook endpoint is secured and verifies Stripe signature

stack.frontend: Plain HTML/CSS/JS (served by Express static middleware)

env_vars.description: Stripe secret key for creating customers and subscriptions
Stripe webhook signing secret for verifying subscription events
Stripe price ID for the Starter plan ($9/mo)
Server port — set automatically by Replit

decision_log.reason: Stack backfilled from existing v0.1 codebase — Node.js + Express + SQLite already implemented and verified working

research_complete.notes: Backfilled from completed research run. Product scored 22/25. Problem, market, and persona validated.

scope_approved.notes: Backfilled from existing v0.1 implementation. Scope matches what was built. 5 features in, 5 out.

mvp_functional.notes: v0.1 codebase verified — core flow works end-to-end. Ingest, sig verification, fanout, retries, event log all present.

ship_approved.notes: Reviewed — mmp scope confirmed, Stripe integration and freemium enforcement look right

mmp_complete.notes: All MMP additions implemented: Stripe checkout flow, usage metering, 429 freemium enforcement, onboarding wizard, updated landing page. 429 path verified with automated test. Server boots cleanly on Replit-compatible stack.

_runs.notes: Spec backfilled manually from existing v0.1 codebase and prior research. Skipping research, mockup, and mvp stages — all work already done.
